title: Warm Apple Tarte Tatin
description: A classic French dessert with a twist, this warm apple tarte tatin is perfect for a special occasion. The combination of tender apples, sweet caramel, and buttery pastry is sure to impress.

ingredients.name: Apples
ingredients.notes: Granny Smith or other tart variety

instructions: Prepare the apples: Peel, halve, and core the apples.
In a large skillet, melt the butter over medium heat.
Add the sugar, flour, and salt to the skillet. Cook, stirring constantly, for 2-3 minutes.
Add the apples to the skillet and cook, stirring occasionally, for 10-12 minutes or until tender.
Roll out the puff pastry to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the pastry to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
Spread the apple mixture evenly over the pastry, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Fold the edges of the pastry up over the apples, pressing gently to seal.
Bake the tarte tatin for 25-30 minutes or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the tarte tatin from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes.
Invert the tarte tatin onto a serving plate and serve warm.
